div1.entryFree:
Qualified, 1) of a quality, of a kind: she is so q. as may beseem the spouse of any noble gentleman, Shr. IV, 5, 66. with thoughts so q. as your charities shall best instruct you, measure me, Wint. II, 1, 113.
2) having a qualification, endowed, fit: that which ordinary men are fit for, I am q. in, Lr. I, 4, 37. more fair, virtuous, wise, chaste, constant, q. Cymb. I, 4, 65 (some M. Edd. constant-qualified).
